cases.description: New rule creation triggers the skill
cases.prompt: create a new rule that all API endpoints must validate with Zod
expectations: Activates rule-creator skill
Interviews user for behavior, language, scope, rationale before writing
Runs `codi add rule <name>` to scaffold
Writes frontmatter with valid YAML (no merged lines)
Produces body with imperative mood + rationale annotations + BAD/GOOD example

cases.description: Team coding standard request triggers the skill
cases.prompt: add a team coding standard for React component file naming
expectations: Activates rule-creator skill
Scopes the rule to React / TypeScript files
Uses language: typescript in frontmatter
Includes measurable criteria (e.g., PascalCase + one component per file)

cases.description: Scaffold from built-in rule template triggers the skill
cases.prompt: use the testing rule template as a starting point for our test standards
expectations: Activates rule-creator skill
Runs `codi add rule testing` (or similar template-based command)
Customizes the scaffolded rule for the user's project
Runs `codi validate` and `codi generate` at the end

cases.description: Skill creation request does NOT trigger rule-creator
cases.prompt: create a new skill that wraps our internal API
expectations: Does NOT activate rule-creator skill
Routes to codi-skill-creator instead
Does NOT write a rule file

cases.description: Request that would duplicate linter behavior is flagged
cases.prompt: make a rule that enforces 2-space indentation
expectations: Activates rule-creator skill
Notes this is already enforced by the formatter / linter
Recommends configuring Prettier/ESLint/Ruff instead of creating a duplicative rule
Only proceeds if the user insists on a rule on top of linter config
